response.justification: Mohammed Al-Said is primarily a web and TypeScript/Node.js engineer with approximately 6 months of C++ work at National Instruments in 2014–2015 — far short of the 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development the role mandates. His only finance-adjacent role (RIDE Capital) was built entirely on Node.js and TypeScript, not C++, and carries no fixed-income or capital-markets system knowledge. There is no evidence of SQL Server or Oracle proficiency, no Summit platform experience, and no exposure to the messaging or low-latency trading infrastructure typical of capital-markets environments.
strengths: Demonstrated software craftsmanship discipline: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development applied consistently across multiple roles
Brief but real C++ exposure at National Instruments writing components for a professional engineering IDE
Strong communication skills evidenced by conference talks, workshops, and open-source community contributions
concerns: C++ experience is limited to roughly 6 months (~2014–2015) and a student thesis project; nowhere near the 5–10 years required, and no indication of Windows-platform C++ work
Zero fixed-income, capital-markets, or investment-banking system knowledge; RIDE Capital used Node.js/TypeScript and focused on retail investment UX, not trading infrastructure
No SQL Server or Oracle experience mentioned; database work is limited to MongoDB and MySQL, which are not the enterprise RDBMS platforms specified as mandatory
